channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: What Happened When Trump Met Xi
item.description: Historian Rana Mitter on the new balance of power between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Who are Europe’s newest troublemakers?
item.description: Corruption and inflation are boosting eurosceptic populists

item.title: G-7 Finance Ministers Discuss Economic Fallout of Iran War
item.description: Rising oil prices and growing bond market volatility have raised fears of a global recession.

item.title: Where expat escapees from Dubai end up
item.description: Will they ever return?

item.title: Iran Is Winning the Vibe War
item.description: Disinformation experts need a new framework in the era of AI slop.

item.title: Ukraine Has a New War Strategy—and It’s Working
item.description: A year ago, the Ukrainian government decided to take the fight directly to Russia. It hasn’t looked back since.

item.title: How much is Donald Trump costing America’s economy?
item.description: We calculate the drag on growth from fitful presidential policymaking

item.title: FIFA’s exorbitant World Cup tickets could backfire
item.description: Why it is hard to monetise football’s atmosphere

item.title: Why Did Trump Visit the Temple of Heaven in Beijing?
item.description: A look back at Beijing’s choices for where to stage diplomatic scenes with U.S. presidents – and the changing political narratives.

item.title: The War Room newsletter: Why the Iran conflict may reignite
item.description: Another contest of wills could end just as badly, writes Shashank Joshi, our defence editor

item.title: What’s on the Agenda for the 3rd Lee-Takaichi Summit?
item.description: Japan and South Korea are seeking to further institutionalize their security, energy, and strategic cooperation – especially after Trump’s trip to Beijing.

item.title: Now it’s Vladimir Putin’s turn to visit Beijing
item.description: Russia’s leader wants a deal to build a gas pipeline to China

item.title: The Trump-Xi Summit’s Nuclear Shadow Over Asia
item.description: Beneath the summit optics lies the reality that China’s rapid nuclear transformation is increasingly straining extended deterrence and unsettling the United States’ Asian allies.

item.title: Brothers in Humiliation
item.description: Confident in the belief that there is nothing their respective militaries cannot do, Vladimir Putin and Donald Trump led their countries into wars that failed virtually from the start. Now, they are looking to each other for help getting out of the messes they made, but neither holds winning cards.

item.title: Iran Could Be Trump’s Greatest Failure
item.description: A lot of things have gone horribly wrong—and it’s not over yet.

item.title: Taiwan’s Special Defense Budget Cut Will Cost Its Drone Capabilities
item.description: The special budget passed by the Legislative Yuan ends all new domestic defense drone procurement at a critical moment in the industry's development.

item.title: The Gulf’s African Aftershocks
item.description: How the Iran war is deepening proxy conflicts around the Red Sea and the Horn of Africa.

item.title: At SCO Security Heads Meeting, Russia Stakes Out ‘Full-fledged Partnership’ With Taliban-ruled Afghanistan
item.description: Russia's Sergei Shoigu also said that "a return of third-country military infrastructure to Afghanistan or the deployment of new military facilities in neighboring states" would be "unacceptable."

item.title: Kim Jong Un Convenes a Military-Wide Commander Meeting
item.description: The North Korean leader called for restructuring the army’s training system and reinforcing front-line units along the southern border, as Pyongyang deepens its push to redefine the security landscape of the Korean Peninsula.

item.title: Mongolia’s Long Struggle to Ensure Accountability in Governance
item.description: Is a new bill the answer, or just the latest iteration of factional infighting in the ruling MPP?

item.title: Trump’s China Opening and Japan’s Taiwan Anxiety
item.description: Tokyo fears being sidelined again as Washington and Beijing recalibrate their relationship. Call it the “Trump Shock.”

item.title: Bridging Geopolitical Divides: Tokayev’s Message at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum
item.description: Tokayev’s remarks at the Antalya Diplomacy Forum underscored Kazakhstan’s ambition to play a larger stabilizing role in global diplomacy.

item.title: Duterte vs Marcos: The Political Feud Tearing Apart the Philippines
item.description: The Duterte-vs-Marcos battle is much more than a domestic political matter; it could determine the fate of the South China Sea.

item.title: Africa Is the Key to Sustained Global Growth
item.description: The rest of the world continues to shunt Africa to the periphery, regarding it mainly as a source of raw materials. But as more advanced economies face structural problems, the continent’s unmatched demographic momentum and enormous industrialization potential imply that it will play a central role in global economic expansion.

item.title: The Root of Today’s Global Imbalances
item.description: Today’s global imbalances are driven primarily by domestic saving-investment dynamics and the interaction between geopolitical rivalry, technological competition, and capital flows. Only by taking coordinated action to manage the associated risks can the world prevent today’s tensions from erupting in another global economic crisis.

item.title: How California Politics Set the National Tone
item.description: Ronald Reagan honed his right-wing conservatism in the Golden State.

item.title: Is Trump selling out Taiwan?
item.description: He suggests he is bargaining with China over weapons sales to the island

item.title: Is Donald Trump selling out Taiwan?
item.description: He suggests he is bargaining with China over weapons sales to the island

item.title: When Volatility Is Good Politics
item.description: The Iran war has revealed how deeply material interests are embedded in strategic calculations, concentrating rewards among powerful insiders while leaving society to absorb the costs. As long as chaos remains politically and economically rewarding, such conflicts will remain difficult to contain.

item.title: Sri Lanka’s Mattala Rajapaksa International Airport Refuses to Disappear
item.description: If Mattala is leased mainly to satisfy strategic anxieties, Colombo risks repeating its old mistake: treating geopolitical value as a substitute for commercial viability.

item.title: There Is a Solution to the Global Health Care Crisis
item.description: Cuts in foreign aid have been devastating. Countries have a window to step in and craft plans for success.

item.title: There Is a Solution to the Global Health Care Crisis
item.description: Cuts in foreign aid have been devastating. Countries have a window to step in and craft plans for success.

item.title: Trump and Xi: The Impact on Southeast Asia
item.description: Bart Édes explains what last week's landmark summit meant for ASEAN and the region's middle powers.

item.title: Philippine Government Urges Supreme Court to Reject Fugitive Senator’s ICC Appeal
item.description: The Office of the Solicitor General ​described Ronald Dela Rosa, who is wanted by The Hague-based court for his role in a bloody anti-drug campaign, as a “fugitive from justice."

item.title: A New Order for the Gulf
item.description: The region must build its own security, not buy it.

item.title: Malaysian Ex-Ministers Resign From PM’s Party, Vacate Parliamentary Seats
item.description: The defection of Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad is likely to amplify rumors of an early general election.

item.title: What’s Next After the Senate Coup in the Philippines?
item.description: Last week's events, including the second impeachment of Vice President Sara Duterte, could further polarize the country's politics ahead of the 2028 presidential election.
